description_long: This object is a roughly leaf-shaped stone with a pointed tip and a broad base, tapering symmetrically along its length. The surface is uneven and textured, with visible chipping and irregularities along the edges, suggesting it may have been shaped or weathered over time. The coloration is a mix of dark gray and brownish hues, with subtle mottling that gives it a natural, earthy appearance. The stone is relatively thin when viewed from the side, with a slight curvature and a rugged profile. There are no visible carvings or artificial markings, and the overall form is organic and unpolished. Its size and shape make it suitable as a small obstacle or decorative element in an urban landscape.
description: A leaf-shaped, rugged stone with a pointed tip, broad base, and mottled gray-brown coloration. The surface is rough and unpolished, with natural chipping along the edges.
description_view_0: Front view shows the full leaf-like outline, with a pointed tip and broad, irregular base.
description_view_1: Side view reveals the stone's thin profile and uneven thickness, highlighting its rugged edge.
description_view_2: Back view mirrors the front, displaying the same pointed and broad shape with visible surface texture.
description_view_3: Opposite side view again shows the thinness and rough, chipped edge of the stone.
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature or minor obstacle on the ground
attribute_pattern: Subtle mottling and irregular chipping, with no regular or artificial patterns
attribute_weathering: Rough and unpolished, with visible edge chipping and natural surface wear
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Blends naturally with soil, gravel, or other stones, providing a subtle contrast to paved surfaces
